sentences.text: Hello!
Hi!
How are you?
Yeah, I… I don't know.
I'm doing well.
Like, overall, I'm doing well, but I thought of you this morning.
I have a puppy who, like, I started my day chasing my dog on the roof, so I'm curious, like, is something in the water this week?
Like, why… Can't we catch a break?
That's a great question.
I don't know.
It started with that, and then, like, work problems started, and I was like, I just… I can't, I can't!
Yeah, that… that's me right now.
I'm… it's just been a crazy couple of weeks over here, and so I'm just… I just feel like I'm always playing catch-up, and… I'm… I'm kind of frustrated with my boss right now, and…
Damn.
It's just one of those things, you know?
I know, I know, which is so… yeah, so frustrating.
I have to ask, because I know that you kind of… Are you at home?
I'm at home, yeah.
Okay, because when you came on, there were… there were, like, some, like, recent shakeups prior to your coming on to AG, right?
Okay.
Huh.
So, has that kind of continued trend-wise a little bit?
Yes.
I think that, well, we have very high turnover, and not just at a site level, like most property management companies do.
I mean, it's at a corporate level, too, and…
Yeah.
There has been a lot of shakeup.
I mean, a lot of our executive leaders, have left over the last 90 days, while…
last 90 days.
So we had… well, we had two.
And they kind of came over together, and so I guess it kind of made sense that they left together, but yeah, they definitely both left.
And then we hired… So…
I mean, we're a small company.
We're only 22 sites, right?
And so, we're kind of boutique-y.
And only in the DFW area.
Right.
So we had…
1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6… like, 7, what you would call, I guess, like, executive leaders, and I felt like that was a lot.
Yeah.
And then we brought another one on, and so.
That is a lot of… what's the expression?
Cooks in the kitchen?
Yeah, so two of them left.
One of them, unfortunately, we do have to replace, because it's our VP over, like, the finance and accounting, and.
Huh?
That's definitely where we struggle the most, as accounting, and so… We need… and we already have what you would call, I guess, an executive leader over, but she doesn't… it's just… it's a mess.
Yeah, okay.
Well, you've… so you've had kind of, like, a hell of a year then, because last time we spoke, you were about to get surgery, which…
What?
Yeah.
Yeah, it has my appendix out.
Or, not my appendix, I'm sorry, my gallbladder.
So you got your gallbladder out, and if I'm remembering correctly, that was, like, weeks after you started this job, right?
One week, yeah, one week after I started.
Yeah, so yeah, it's been pretty crazy.
I… I… I don't know.
It's… and, you know, last year was even crazier.
Honestly, Shelby, I had a really crazy year last year, and I didn't work.
At all last year.
Yeah.
So, it was just…
Yeah, it's just been…
I know, I know.
Yeah, it just feels like the last, like, 5 years, it's just like… If it's not one thing, it's another.
If it's not something personally, it's something with the economy.
It's exhausting!
But yeah, you… okay, because.
I'm trying to remember… was it Bellrock?
What was the company that you were?
Fair enough.
Holy, you're… you hit it right there.
So yeah, that was the first time we met.
So, yeah, well, cheers.
Here we are in the year, 2025.
Yeah.
Well, I'm glad that your surgery went well.
Thanks.
Yeah.
Yes, and then… I'm trying to think, yeah, I got engaged right after our last call.
I know.
Are you… do you have a date?
No, Jessica, I'm so, like, Type B.
It's really bad.
I'm just like… it'll… like, personally, I, I don't think I'm, like, interested in having children.
I never really have wanted that, so I kind of feel like, you know, what's time?
We're just… but no, I think it's… now I'm like, okay, we should probably start.
I think we're gonna do Morocco for the wedding.
Yep, my fiance's, family, he has family, in Morocco, so that feels like a fun… I don't know, like a… Probably a way, honestly, to keep costs lower.
Honestly, yeah.
Yeah.
Just do it, you know, destination, it makes everything easy.
Do y'all… either of y'all have big families?
So, what I… so my fiance's in hospitality, where I don't think we're gonna have a lot of, like, an overwhelming amount of family.
So many friends.
And I have, like, 5 friends, so I'm like, this is gonna be really funny to, like, see…
So, I think we're gonna, like, I think we're aiming for Spring 27, and I think we're gonna go in November to, like, scout it out.
There you.
So, we got some time, but yeah, the fact that we were chatting in March, after I just got engaged, and I still have no updates, I'm just…
That's okay.
We're trucking along.
That's alright, you know, you take that Type B, and you just run with it, girl.
I wish that I could be more like that.
Yes, yes, it is a blessing and a curse, as you can imagine.
So, anyway, well, it's so good to see you.
Yes, cheers to us surviving this far in the year.
And I know that y'all have Sochi in place, so, like, it's likely that this'll be, like, a quick little catch-up.
Remind me, did you sign a 2-year contract with Sochi?
No, I only signed one year, so we're… we're stuck with them through July of 26.
Okay.
And I say stuck, I don't mean stuck.
I mean, you know, we are locked in with them until 26.
Yep.
I will say I'm not as happy with SOCI this go-around, as I have been, because I had it with Sunridge, and then I had it at Bell Rock.

So, with Sunridge, I came into them already having SOCI, and, like, they were, like, long-time SOCI, clients, so… it was like, you've got to learn this, and I really… I was the marketing coordinator, so I was basically, like, a glorified marketing assistant, and SoC was, like, my lifeline.
Like, that is where I did everything.
Right, yeah.

I went over to Bellrock, and I was the marketing director, and I was a one-man show, and I only had 7 properties, not 150, and So, I wanted to incorporate Soci, because, you know, I think social media is kind of like the future of marketing.
I mean, it is marketing now, but it's also kind of where everybody is headed, right?
And so, I just needed somewhere, I needed a platform, like a one-stop shop for all things social media, and we had nothing in place.
Everybody was kind of going in and just making posts, and… I don't like that.
I… I guess I'm a control freak, because, like, I want to be able to approve posts, I want to see them before they go out.
Totally.
I mean, that's totally valid.
You're definitely not alone in that.
That makes sense.
And so, I onboarded us with Societ Bellrock, which was fine, but it was very short-lived, because I was laid off, like.
less than… 4 months after we had onboarded SOCI, so… I didn't really get to even…
Yeah.
like, have that experience as, like, you know, like, the sole ownership over it.
And then I went ahead and brought it on board here, because we were more than 7 properties, we were 22.
Yeah.
And I mean, when I say everything here has been a nightmare and a little bit of damage control, like.
Yeah.
I do not say that lightly.
It's been…
Ugh, Jessica, I'm so sorry.
You have had, like, a hell of a 3 years, then.
Yeah, yeah, it has been, and you know, it's like.
Do I want to stay?
Do I want to go?
I don't know.
Like, I see a lot of potential with this company, but it's… it's gonna be a hard road.
We just onboarded with Elise AI.
I've been handling all of that, because… my teens… and, you know, we're Class B and C properties, so we're… we're kind of… where do you live?
Are you in Austin?
I'm in Austin now, yeah.
So, like, you know, like, East Austin, anything, like, east of 35 that's, like, mmm, a little rough around the edges, that's… those are my sites.
I'm not luxury anymore.
Like, Bellrock was luxury.
Sunridge was kind of a mixture of both.

But the ones that were, like, Class BC, like, value-add properties, I didn't do a lot of marketing for, so it was mostly, like, the lease-ups and, like, the, you know, Class A properties that I really focused on, so… because we are all Class BC properties, and some of them I mean, I was on the phone with one of the managers the other day, and she had, like, some guy that was all cracked out, trying to bathe in the pool, and she's yelling at him, like, you need to get, like, she's like, you need to get your ass out of this pool now.
And she's yelling at this guy.
Oh, wow.
So, those are things that, like, our managers deal with on a day-to-day.
So, with all of that being said, I love the teams that we have in place, but they are a little rough around the edges.

And so, you know, it's like making sure that they're using, like, ChatGPT, and making sure that they've got Grammarly downloaded on their computers, and social media makes me very, very nervous, and so…
you know, it's just one of those things that it's a little bit overwhelming, and I have to do a lot of hand-holding.
And… we don't really have a lot of funding to spend on, like, mass marketing, so it's like, I have to be very selective in what I choose.
And so, with all of that being said, I almost regret Soci, because…
it's one of those things, I don't even have any of the teams using it right now.
I have a social media guy, he's offshore, he lives in the Philippines.
Okay, I remember, and that's someone, he was on the team when you came on board, right?
Wasn't that…
Marco, yes, and he… I tried… I tried to replace him, I interviewed some people, they wouldn't let me get anybody here in the States.
I had to interview other people in the Philippines, or offshore.
And it… I just… I said, you know what?
I won't… I'm not a good manager if I just, like, want to immediately replace, like, I have to work with them.
And so I've been with them for about 6 months now, and… things have gotten better, and they've gotten worse.
I mean, it's kind of both, but he has really, truly, like, taken SOCI, and he is doing a pretty good job with it.
I mean, he's, you know, actively posting, and I only have 12 sites on it right now.
I don't have 10 of my sites.
We're… we're onboarding in, like, 20 days, so…
Okay.
Yeah, just…
Okay, so… so wait, the 12 sites, you said those 12 are currently being onboarded, or you're onboarding?
They're live on Societ, but, like, we did, like, a three-tier rollout.
So I did, like, the first 6 properties, and then I did 6 more, and now we're doing our last 10, finally.
Okay.
So, yeah, it's been kind of a long process.

And when I say, like, the experience hasn't been as great, this is kind of what I mean, like, I still don't have surveys set up, I still don't have, like, my listings complete, and, like, I'm not really being offered a whole lot of support there, and because I.
Yeah.
Like, I can't hand that off to Marco.

You know, that's just not something that… I'm comfortable with him doing, and so when I… when I say I have not lived in SOCI for more than, like, 3 hours total combined over the last, like, 3 or 4 months, I'm…
Yeah.
I'm very serious when I say that.
I go in there and I approve his posts, and I will sometimes respond to reviews, but we pay RealPage to respond to our reviews already, so it's like…
covered.
Like, I'm just not even… It's just…
Okay.
I need to get it together, is what I need to do.
It doesn't sound like you need to get it together, it just sounds like, yeah, there's a lot of… Like, moving parts that…
Okay.
So, with that being said, because I know that… I was like, well, no, is Marco the one that is posting on social media, or do some of the site managers also?
Nope, it's just Marco.
I have not onboarded, so… with AG… Because everything has been a dumpster fire.
Literally.
I mean, the property was… truly just ran into the ground, not just from a marketing component, like.
from, like, an operational.
And so we have had, since I've been put on board, I mean, we've had so many new policies.

put in place, tons of rollouts, and so, you know, the big one right now is Elise AI, and so… I didn't want to train the teams to use SOCI until after I had all 22 sites on SOCI anyways, and so I was planning on rolling it out in October, but it looks like I'm going to have to push that to November, so I won't even have teams start posting, and when they do start posting, it's only going to be, like, once a week.
It's not going to be… Like, a full… time, let's post, you know, 3 or 4 times a week.
It's gonna be… yeah.
Right.
Okay, okay, got it.

So, I guess, just because, since we have spoke, we have… I mean, I know that we chatted about the reputation and listings, Sochi not really being able to integrate with RealPage, that will always kind of be a little bit more of a manual lift.
Listings is completely a manual lift, and during the implementation phase, they did help me with it.
There's, like.
what they call, like, a location information something or another that you fill in, and they kind of go in and do it all for you.
Okay.
But it's very, very basic, so anything extra, any kind of added SEO or keyword or you know, data, it is a manual lift, and that's something that I have not touched.
And so…
Because now, like, so we can obviously, as you know, we automate Google, reputation and listings, that's automated with us as well.
And then we now have this really pretty social AI dashboard.
The biggest thing that, like.
just… So, so the way that it works is we will automate postings, you know, property-specific postings to both Facebook and Instagram twice a week.
Like, we found that Tuesdays and Thursdays, those are the highest trafficked times.
So we do that, and we're actually gonna start rolling out Localized content as well.
So…
like, nearby restaurants, like, we will make a post about.
So, like, as far as automation goes, like, it sounds like you know, I… the biggest thing, going back to, like, what I know was important to you is TikTok.
Is that still the case?
Are you kind of, like, geared more towards, like.
how much can I take off my team… myself and my team's plate?
So, TikTok is… was kind of tabled, We do have… I do have some teams that have created a TikTok account.
People that…
you know, it's just, it's really hard to monitor TikTok.
SoC does connect to our TikTok, I will say that much.
Yes.
Which is nice.
But… I… I… I… I do not have the bandwidth to monitor TikTok.
I just don't.
And now I've got… So, our training gal, Tria, she… For whatever reason or another, we hired two floating leasers, to kind of just bounce from property to property to help out where needed.
And the first task that she assigned them to was to go in and take videos of, like, vacant units, so we would have, like, virtual libraries.
Yeah.

So, now she wants me to create a YouTube channel, so we can upload all of them to this YouTube channel, so we can have links to the videos, because… they won't send through email, and, it's hard to send through Dropbox, and it's hard to send through Google Drive.
Yeah.
And so, I'm like, well, that's just one more thing that I get to monitor, like.
Right.
It's literally just dumped in my lap, and I'm like, can I think about this?
Like, I'm not sure that that's what I want to do, but…
Right.
So is that… is… are the YouTube videos meant for social media, or is that for, like, virtual leasing?
Like, if someone's looking at…
Virtual leasing, yeah, so it's meant for, you know, like, our leasing agents to be able to send a link to, like, hey, if you can't make it, here's a video of one of our vacants.
Got it.
to look through it, but… You know, and this is just me coming from luxury.
I'm like, I want those professional.
I don't want them done on an iPhone, but we can't afford it, you know?
So I'm like, she's like, it's better than nothing, and I'm like, I guess.
Yeah, right, yeah, okay, okay, alright, Jessica.
I'm so sorry, you have a law on your plate right now.

So, okay, I'm curious… And, you know, I understand, but given how automated our product suite is, would you be open to rolling out those other 8 properties with us, or is there, like, an agreement with Sochi.
I already have an agreement with Sophie.
Okay.
Sadly.
Yeah, I do.
Okay, alright, got it.
Well then in that case… Yeah, I won't… I will save you the full spiel, but, I guess… I would love, you know, obviously, I'd love to stay in touch.
I love chatting with you every time.
Yeah, I know.
Good to see you.
But yeah, I guess with that being said, like, do we just keep in touch, or, like, maybe revisit… like, Q2 of next year, as y'all are closer to that July date, or…
Yeah, I think that,
I mean, I have not forgotten about you.
I want you to know that I haven't forgotten about you.
You have been, like, amazing with follow-up.
So, I think that Q2 would probably be a good time… would be a good time to revisit.
And then… God, hopefully I have my shit together by then.
I mean, no, it doesn't… first of all, it doesn't sound like it's you.
It sounds like, yeah, I mean… and that's the thing with marketing and, like, why this product suite exists, blah blah blah, because we… we know that, like.
Marketing folks get tasked with, like.
so many things that aren't really in your wheelhouse.
Like, for instance, I don't think those YouTube videos should really fall in your wheelhouse.
Like, that feels…
Like, yeah, so anyway, yeah, it's definitely not you.
I am sorry that you are being asked of… yeah, so much is being asked of you.
But okay, well then, yeah, we can just continue to keep in touch.
I am really… yeah, I'll be really excited to show you everything.
The dashboard's been really great, and then at that point, the one thing that I, like, honestly think is lacking from our dashboard is some sort of, like.

for those manual posts, if you did want to give your on-site teams or Marco some level of access, I think that something that we are working on right now that will be beneficial is having some sort of, like, approval process in place for when those Manual posts are created.
But yeah, otherwise… reputation, listings, like, that's all automated.
Our listings tool just rolled out, like, a feature that allows you to see which SEO keywords will help you rank higher in the area, which is really neat.
So, Yes, you're always the sweetest.
I appreciate you, like, taking the time to even chat, even though, like, obviously it doesn't make sense for y'all to bring on anything right now.
But yeah, I guess we'll just, like… Continue to chit-chat from afar.
Please.
And I will be thinking of you, because you're such a trooper.
Yeah, well, you know, as long as we get a lease rolled out, and there are no major hiccups.
Which, by the way, I've heard great things about Elise AI.
Like, everything that I've heard from Elise AI is truly great, so I'm really excited for y'all to roll her out, for lack of a better word.
I have on 5 properties, and so far, everything's been great.
My teams are loving it.
I know that they're… we're going to centralize.
We're centralizing everything, so we're moving to, like, a fully integrated, fully centralized, company, and I have this vision, and it's, like.
I think that we can do great things.
I'm kind of pining for, like, the head of centralization role, so I can kind of…
Yeah!

you know, move over, and not do marketing anymore, just because I don't think that the marketing department is right for me, maybe, and I just… I see things in, like, big picture, and so I think it's more of, like, operational mindset, it seems, yeah.
So, you know, and it's something that I've discussed with our COO, is, you know, like, would… could that be me?
But we have so many people, kind of.

vying, I think, for that role, that it's like, maybe… and… and nobody wants… nobody, like, I've been told nobody can do marketing like me, so… we… we could never replace you, like, how do we replace you?
But I'm like, well, does that… is that really an appropriate response?
Because it's kind of holding me back?
Right.
Like, because I'm too good at my job, that doesn't make sense.
No.

So, I don't know, it's just… it's a lot, and, you know, we're kind of just… all over the place, and I'm, you know, just thinking out loud all the time, like, well, maybe we can just centralize marketing, and I could bring in, like, a full-time assistant in the office, and not an assistant over in the Philippines.
And they can handle, like, the day-to-day, but I could still handle, like.
Billing and accounting, and like, you know, like, making sure our accounts are good, and contracts are good, and they can handle, like, the social media side, and…
Yeah.
making flyers, which I don't do anyways anymore, but, you know, like, having somebody in the office like that, I think, would be…
Absolutely.
Much better use of time.
Yeah, and like, no offense to Margo, I'm sure he's incredible at his job, but yeah, at the end of the day, like.
A lot of the times, like, especially with multifamily, like, you kind of have to be on the ground, you know, like, yeah.
Taking…
those virtual tours and whatnot.
Yeah, yeah.
Yeah, and so it's just… it's one of those things that, like, I'm trying to, like, be patient and just continue doing my job, but then… this isn't recorded, is it?
It is.
But that's fine.
I mean, this… this will not go anywhere.
No, it's okay.
